mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the housing market (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals a surge in consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average of the S&P 500 over the same period (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over the next 60 trading days signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 60-day moving average of the S&P 500 over the same period (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 20 trading days signals a tightening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average of the S&P 500 over the same period (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the housing market (HOUST) and a 10% increase in real personal income (RSAFS) over the next 5 trading days signal a surge in consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average of the S&P 500 over the same period (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the next 20 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average of the S&P 500 over the same period (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 5% decline in the housing market (HOUST) over the next 60 trading days signal a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 60-day moving average of the S&P 500 over the same period (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a 0.5% increase in the labor force participation rate (PAYEMS) over the next 20 trading days signal a tightening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average of the S&P 500 over the same period (t-test p < 0.05).
